rules.sourceTitle: FAO/WHO Codex Alimentarius
rules.notes: Automated extraction failed: {"error":{"code":503,"message":"The service is currently unavailable.","status":"UNAVAILABLE"}}

rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Verification of approved additives and their conditions of use is required via the Additives Database, which is based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Food safety criteria for bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Compliance with food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins, and metabolites in specific foods is required as laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: Listeria monocytogenes sampling in processing areas and equipment
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Food business operators manufacturing RTE food with a potential L. monocytogenes risk must perform sampling procedures on food processing areas and equipment surfaces according to guidelines.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Guidelines on sampling the food processing areas and equipment describes sampling procedures to be performed by food business operators manufacturing RTE food which may pose a L. monocytogenes risk for public health in order to detect L. monocytogenes on the surfaces of RTE food processing areas and equipment.

rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Shelf-life studies (durability studies and challenge tests) for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods must be conducted according to technical guidance and comply with food safety criteria in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Technical guidance document on shelf-life studies for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods provides specialised laboratories with detailed and practical information on how to conduct shelf-life studies... in order to comply with the food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Compliance with process hygiene criteria is required to indicate the correct functioning of the production process, as laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: In addition, this Regulation lays down certain process hygiene criteria to indicate the correct functioning of the production process.

rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Other EU countries or regions can obtain special Salmonella guarantees if their control programme is recognized as equivalent to those approved for Sweden and Finland, in accordance with Regulations (EC) No 853/2004 (foodstuffs) and 2160/2003 (animals).
rules.evidenceQuote: Other EU countries or region of EU countries can obtain special guarantees if it has a control programme recognised as equivalent to that approved for Sweden and Finland in accordance with Regulations (EC) No 853/2004 (foodstuffs) and 2160/2003 (animals).

rules.foodCategory: Certain live animals and products (e.g., poultry, meat, eggs)
rules.parameter: Salmonella prevalence (special guarantees for Sweden, Finland, Norway)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Special guarantees regarding Salmonella prevalence apply to trade of certain live animals and products from other countries to Sweden, Finland, and Norway, as laid down in specific Council Decisions and Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: When Sweden and Finland joined the EU special guarantees on Salmonella were provided as regards trade from other countries of certain live animals and products... The current guarantees are laid down in: Council Decision 95/410/EC... Decision 2004/235/EC... Decision 2003/644/EC... Regulation (EC) No 1688/2005...

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for pre-packaged foodstuffs
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية - بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This regulation covers general labelling requirements for pre-packaged foodstuffs, which typically include ingredient lists, allergen declarations, nutritional information, and warnings. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The 'Substances Added to Food inventory' includes these types of ingredients, with specific regulations in 21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74, 182, 184, and 181.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*. Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184). Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.limit: No established threshold level for any allergens.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: A threshold is a value below which it is unlikely that a food allergic individual would experience an adverse effect.
rules.evidenceQuote: At this time, the FDA has not established a threshold level for any allergens.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. FALCPA requires specific labeling.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.limit: Must meet defined standard for gluten content.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Specific compliance requirements exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim, effective August 12, 2020.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling, which helps consumers, especially those living with celiac disease, be confident that items labeled “gluten-free” meet a defined standard for gluten content.

rules.limit: Not required by law; must be truthful and not misleading; not a substitute for GMPs.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Such statements are used to address unavoidable 'cross-contact' after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated.
rules.evidenceQuote: Consumers may also see advisory statements such as “may contain [allergen] or “produced in a facility that also uses [allergen].” Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact

rules.limit: Facilities must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate labeling.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Part of the FDA’s “Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food” rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.limit: Must appear in parentheses after ingredient name OR in a 'contains' statement immediately after/next to the ingredient list.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The type of tree nut, species of fish, and Crustacean shellfish must also be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label in one of two ways. The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient... OR Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present in food at or above the specified concentration due to potential adverse reactions.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.notes: Specific regulations regarding food additive limits for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods must be verified through the official government document database.
